rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. TONY FU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross-Defendant Charles Li Demurrer To Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 3, 2007, LINE 15, Cross-Defendant Charles Li Demurrer To Cross-Complaint. DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT OF FU IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN 20 DAYS AS TO FIRST THROUGH FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THE FACTS THAT ARE TRUE, THE MISREPRESENTATIONS MADE AS TO THOSE FACTS, AND HOW FU WAS INDUCED TO ACT AS A CONSEQUENCE OF THE MISREPRESENTATIONS. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THE FACTS THAT WERE CONCEALED. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD THAT THERE WAS A PROMISE MADE BY LI WITHOUT AN INTENT TO PERFORM, THAT FU RELIED ON THE PROMISE AND AS A CONSEQUENCE WAS DAMAGED. FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST PLEAD FACTS THAT LI OWED FU A DUTY OF CARE, THAT LI BREACHED THE DUTY, THEREBY INJURING FU. FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION: MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR THE CLAIM OF EXTORTION. OVERRULED AS TO SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION. FU ALLEGES A CONTRACT, LI'S BREACH BY FAILING TO MAKE REASONABLE COLLECTION EFFORTS THEREBY CAUSING FU DAMAGE. 20 DAYS TO ANSWER. =(302/PJM/AL)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. TONY FU et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 3, 2007, LINE 15, DEFENDANT TONY FU'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER LI KNEW ABOUT 2002 TERMINATION AGREEMENT. =(302/PJM/AL)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. VENUS WILLIAMS et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, OCTOBER 3, 2007, LINE 14, PLAINTIFF SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE MOTION IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT DEFENDANT COMMITTED A PUBLIC NUISANCE AND HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE ACTS IN THE NOTICE WERE COMMITTED BY DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF HAS ESTABLISHED ONLY THAT A STAY AWAY ORDER WAS ISSUED AGAINST DEFENDANT AFTER A COURT HEARING. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO POLICE REPORTS B, C, D, J AND O ARE SUSTAINED ON THE GROUNDS OF HEARSAY. THE OBJECTION TO I IS OVERRULED. =(302/PJM/AY)
